general_skills.title: Verify Before Mutating
general_skills.principle: Always read the current state of an Odoo record before creating, updating, or deleting it. Confirm the operation with the user when it affects production data.
general_skills.application: Before any write/create/delete call, do a search_read to confirm the target exists and is in the expected state. Ask for explicit user confirmation on destructive operations.

general_skills.title: Use Domain Filters Precisely
general_skills.principle: Construct Odoo domains with the most specific filters possible to avoid retrieving excessive data or acting on wrong records.
general_skills.application: Always include state, type, and date filters when querying models like account.move, stock.picking, or sale.order. Prefer exact matches over broad searches.

general_skills.title: Check Model Availability
general_skills.principle: Not all Odoo models are available on every instance — modules may not be installed. Verify model existence before querying.
general_skills.application: Use odoo_list_models or odoo_get_fields to confirm the model exists. If a model is missing, explain which module needs to be installed.

general_skills.title: Batch Operations Safely
general_skills.principle: When processing multiple records, work in small batches and report progress. Never process unbounded result sets in a single call.
general_skills.application: Limit search_read results with a reasonable limit (50-100). For write operations on many records, process in chunks of 10-20 and confirm intermediate results.

general_skills.title: Interpret Odoo Errors Contextually
general_skills.principle: Odoo errors often contain technical tracebacks. Translate them into actionable explanations for the user, identifying the root cause and suggesting fixes.
general_skills.application: When an RPC call fails, parse the error message for common patterns (access rights, missing fields, constraint violations) and explain in plain language.

general_skills.title: Respect the Odoo Workflow
general_skills.principle: Odoo records follow state machines (draft -> confirmed -> done). Never skip states by directly writing state fields — use the appropriate action methods.
general_skills.application: Use action_post for invoices, action_confirm for sale orders, button_validate for stock pickings. Never write {'state': 'posted'} directly.

general_skills.title: Progressive Field Discovery
general_skills.principle: When working with unfamiliar models or investigating data quality, use odoo_get_fields first to understand available fields before querying records.
general_skills.application: Start with odoo_get_fields to see field types and relationships, then construct targeted search_read queries with only the fields needed for analysis.

general_skills.title: Multi-Angle Investigation
general_skills.principle: For thorough analysis, examine the same issue from multiple perspectives using related models and cross-references.
general_skills.application: When checking module states, also query ir.module.module.dependency for dependency issues. When auditing cron jobs, check both ir.cron and ir.logging for execution history.

general_skills.title: Combine Diagnostic Tools Strategically
general_skills.principle: Use odoo_doctor as the initial broad sweep, then follow up with targeted model queries to drill into specific findings. This two-phase approach is more efficient than single-tool queries.
general_skills.application: Start with odoo_doctor to get a system overview. Based on its output, use odoo_search_read on specific models (ir.cron, ir.module.module, ir.logging) to investigate flagged areas in detail.

general_skills.title: Aggregate and Summarize for Clarity
general_skills.principle: When presenting findings from multiple queries, organize results into categorized tables or summaries (OK / Warning / Critical) to help users quickly identify priorities.
general_skills.application: Group related findings by severity level. Present counts, totals, and key metrics in tabular format. Highlight critical items first, then warnings, then informational items.

general_skills.title: Temporal Filtering for Audit Trails
general_skills.principle: When investigating recent events or changes, use date/time filters to narrow scope and improve query performance. Always specify time windows for audit queries.
general_skills.application: Use create_date, write_date, or custom date fields with comparison operators (>, <, >=, <=). For 24-hour lookbacks, calculate datetime.now() - timedelta(hours=24). Include timezone awareness in date comparisons.

general_skills.title: Leverage search_count for Efficiency
general_skills.principle: When you only need to know the quantity of records matching a condition, use search_count instead of search_read to reduce data transfer and improve performance.
general_skills.application: Use odoo_search_count to get record counts before deciding whether to fetch full details. Useful for reporting metrics, detecting anomalies (e.g., 'are there any negative stock records?'), and validating data thresholds.

general_skills.title: Combine Count and Detail Queries Strategically
general_skills.principle: Use search_count first to assess scope, then conditionally fetch details only if counts warrant investigation. This reduces unnecessary data transfer.
general_skills.application: Query search_count with a filter to get magnitude of issue. If count is high, fetch details with limit and offset. If count is zero, report 'no issues found' immediately without additional queries.

general_skills.title: Cross-Reference Related Models for Completeness
general_skills.principle: When investigating a specific model (e.g., ir.cron), always check related audit/logging models (ir.logging) to get the full picture of recent activity and failures.
general_skills.application: For cron jobs, query both ir.cron (state) and ir.logging (execution history). For modules, query both ir.module.module (state) and ir.module.module.dependency (dependency health). This reveals hidden issues.

general_skills.title: Build Dependency Graphs Iteratively
general_skills.principle: When exploring dependency relationships (e.g., module dependencies), query parent-child relationships iteratively rather than trying to fetch the entire tree at once.
general_skills.application: Start with a target module, fetch its direct dependencies via ir.module.module.dependency. For each dependency, recursively fetch its dependencies. Use a visited set to avoid cycles. Present results as a hierarchical structure or adjacency list.

general_skills.title: Validate Data Consistency Across Related Models
general_skills.principle: When investigating module or system state, cross-validate findings across multiple related models to detect inconsistencies that indicate deeper issues.
general_skills.application: For module checks: verify state in ir.module.module matches expected installation status, check ir.module.module.dependency for broken links, and query ir.model for orphaned model definitions. Report any mismatches as warnings.

general_skills.title: Optimize Query Sequences with Early Termination
general_skills.principle: Structure diagnostic queries to fail fast: use search_count to detect zero-result conditions before fetching full records, avoiding unnecessary data transfer.
general_skills.application: When investigating a category of issues (e.g., error logs), first search_count to determine if any records exist. If count is 0, report immediately. If count is high, fetch with limit. This pattern reduces wasted queries.

general_skills.title: Use odoo_model_info for Configuration Discovery
general_skills.principle: When working with system configuration (defaults, settings, field properties), use odoo_model_info to understand model structure before querying or modifying records.
general_skills.application: Call odoo_model_info on models like ir.default, ir.ui.view, ir.actions.act_window to understand their schema. This reveals available fields and relationships needed for targeted queries or modifications.

general_skills.title: Leverage odoo_execute for Computed Insights
general_skills.principle: When standard search/read queries cannot answer a question (e.g., filestore size estimation, complex aggregations), use odoo_execute to run custom logic or SQL.
general_skills.application: Use odoo_execute for: calculating totals across related records, estimating storage from attachment metadata, running database-level aggregations, or executing custom methods. Always validate results with follow-up queries.

general_skills.title: Combine Multiple Count Queries for Comprehensive Metrics
general_skills.principle: When assessing system state (e.g., data volume, pending operations), run multiple search_count queries in sequence to build a complete picture without fetching full records.
general_skills.application: For backup planning: count partners, invoices, orders, pickings, attachments in one turn. For module health: count installed modules, pending upgrades, broken dependencies. Present as a summary table with all metrics.

task_specific_skills.title: Estimate Backup Size via Attachment Analysis
task_specific_skills.principle: To estimate database and filestore size for backup planning, analyze ir.attachment records comprehensively, including file sizes, types, and related data.
task_specific_skills.application: Query ir.attachment with search_count to get total count. Use search_read to fetch records with file_size, type, and create_date fields. Cross-reference with ir.model to understand which models store attachments. Use odoo_execute to sum file sizes if needed. Report total attachment count, total size, and breakdown by model.

task_specific_skills.title: Verify Pre-Restore System State
task_specific_skills.principle: Before a restore operation, verify the database is in a safe state by checking for pending module operations, scheduled jobs, and active sessions.
task_specific_skills.application: Check ir.module.module for state != 'installed' (pending upgrades). Query ir.cron for active jobs that might interfere. Search ir.logging for recent errors. Verify no users are currently logged in via ir.session.session. Report any blockers or warnings.

task_specific_skills.title: Gather Core Data Metrics for Backup Documentation
task_specific_skills.principle: Collect key record counts and system metadata to document the backup state and aid in restore validation.
task_specific_skills.application: Use search_count on core models: res.partner, account.move, sale.order, stock.picking, ir.attachment. Query odoo_doctor for version and database name. Combine results into a summary report with timestamp. This serves as a baseline for post-restore verification.

task_specific_skills.title: Set Global Defaults via ir.default
task_specific_skills.principle: Use ir.default model to set field-level defaults that apply globally across the system, avoiding hardcoding defaults in code.
task_specific_skills.application: Query ir.default with search_read to see existing defaults. To set a new default: create a record with fields_id (field reference), model_id (target model), and user_id (empty for global). Use odoo_model_info to find the correct field ID. Verify with follow-up search_read.

task_specific_skills.title: Modify List View Sort Order via Window Actions
task_specific_skills.principle: Customize list view display order by modifying ir.actions.act_window context to set default sort fields without editing XML.
task_specific_skills.application: Use odoo_modify_action to update the context of a window action. Set context['sort'] to field name and direction (e.g., 'date_order desc'). Verify the change by querying the action's context field. Test in the UI to confirm sort order applies.

task_specific_skills.title: Discover and Validate Field Defaults Before Modification
task_specific_skills.principle: Before setting defaults, use odoo_get_fields and odoo_model_info to understand field types, constraints, and existing defaults.
task_specific_skills.application: Call odoo_get_fields on the target model to list all fields. For each field of interest, check its type (selection, many2one, etc.) and required status. Query ir.default to see if a default already exists. This prevents setting invalid defaults.

common_mistakes.mistake: Querying large result sets without limits
common_mistakes.consequence: Slow responses, high memory usage, potential timeouts. User experience degradation.
common_mistakes.prevention: Always include limit parameter in search_read (50-100 records). Use search_count first to assess result size. For large datasets, use offset and pagination.

common_mistakes.mistake: Modifying state fields directly instead of using workflow actions
common_mistakes.consequence: Bypasses validation, triggers, and state machine logic. Data inconsistency and broken workflows.
common_mistakes.prevention: Always use action methods (action_post, action_confirm, button_validate). Never write state directly. Check GS-006 for correct patterns.

common_mistakes.mistake: Assuming all Odoo models exist on every instance
common_mistakes.consequence: RPC errors when querying unavailable models. Wasted turns debugging non-existent models.
common_mistakes.prevention: Use odoo_list_models or odoo_get_fields to verify model existence before querying. Explain which module is needed if model is missing.

common_mistakes.mistake: Fetching full records when only counts are needed
common_mistakes.consequence: Unnecessary data transfer, slower responses, higher API load.
common_mistakes.prevention: Use search_count for metric queries. Reserve search_read for when record details are actually needed. Apply GS-012 and GS-013.

common_mistakes.mistake: Investigating issues from a single angle without cross-referencing related models
common_mistakes.consequence: Incomplete analysis, missed root causes, incorrect recommendations.
common_mistakes.prevention: Always check related models (e.g., ir.logging for cron execution, ir.module.module.dependency for module issues). Apply GS-008 and GS-014.

common_mistakes.mistake: Ignoring field types and constraints when setting defaults or modifying records
common_mistakes.consequence: Invalid data, constraint violations, silent failures or cryptic errors.
common_mistakes.prevention: Use odoo_get_fields and odoo_model_info to understand field types before modification. Validate field values match expected types (selection options, many2one IDs, etc.).

common_mistakes.mistake: Not confirming user intent before destructive operations
common_mistakes.consequence: Accidental data loss, user frustration, potential compliance issues.
common_mistakes.prevention: Always ask for explicit confirmation before delete/write operations on production data. Show what will be affected. Apply GS-001.

common_mistakes.mistake: Using vague or overly broad domain filters
common_mistakes.consequence: Queries return wrong records, leading to incorrect analysis or unintended modifications.
common_mistakes.prevention: Include specific filters (state, type, date ranges). Prefer exact matches. Test domains with search_count first. Apply GS-002.
